description:
src/lib.rs:96-112 distinguishes the 'slash in secret name' case from the 'missing secret name' case and emits a specific error message ('azure-kv secret name must not contain \'/\\'') instead of a generic 'invalid URI'. This is the right shape — Azure KV secret names cannot contain slashes, and a user with a slash will not figure that out from a generic error.

A future contributor might suggest collapsing this into a single error message for 'simplicity'. Defend it: the duplicate logic is the diagnostic, and one of the most common URI-construction mistakes is treating a vault-path as if it were nested. Worth keeping.
title: Good: actionable error message for nested-secret-name URIs
